metadata.title: Precoil EMT Pattern Library for Steps 7-8
metadata.scope: DVF category definitions, assumption format rules, EMT phase structure, mapping matrix, experiment brief template, and cross-cutting rules for Risk Mapper and PDSA Experiment Designer nodes
metadata.methodology: Extract → Map → Test (EMT) by David J. Bland, Precoil

categories.name: Desirability
categories.scope: User needs, problem severity, perceived value, solution fit only
categories.never_includes: Pricing, dollar amounts, willingness to pay, or any financial assumptions

categories.name: Viability
categories.scope: All financial assumptions: pricing, willingness to pay, revenue, margins, unit economics, business model sustainability

categories.name: Feasibility
categories.scope: Operational, technical, or organizational delivery assumptions

rules: Every assumption must start with 'I believe...' or 'We believe...'
Each assumption should describe something that could prove the idea wrong if tested
Prefer assumptions about observable behavior rather than opinions
Avoid assumptions about total market size, industry growth, or macro trends unless they directly affect early adoption or revenue
Each assumption should represent a distinct risk — do not repeat the same idea across categories

emt_phases.execution_rule: Run phases in sequence. Do not skip ahead. After each phase, ask if the user wants to continue to the next.

phases.name: Extract
phases.purpose: Surface DVF assumptions from the idea
phases.output: 3 assumptions per category (D, V, F) in table format + DVF tension check

output_format.dvf_tension_check: 1-2 sentence description of the most significant tension between assumptions across categories

phases.name: Map
phases.purpose: Critique the user's assumption map on a 2×2 matrix
phases.input: User's assumption placement on Importance (vertical) × Evidence (horizontal) matrix
assessment_criteria: Placement — are assumptions in sensible positions?
DVF coverage — are all three categories represented?
Format — do assumptions follow 'I believe...' format?
Misplacements — flag any assumptions in the wrong quadrant
Priority — identify 1-2 assumptions nearest high importance / low evidence
What was done well — at least one strength

phases.name: Test
phases.purpose: Identify what to test and how, then surface the library ceiling
phases.input: 1-2 riskiest assumptions from high importance / low evidence quadrant
phases.output: Structured experiment brief per assumption + experiment card recommendations from the 44-card library

axes.vertical: Importance (how much damage if this assumption is wrong)

quadrants.label: Test first
quadrants.priority: Highest — these are the riskiest assumptions

sections: How to Run It (3 steps: preparation, execution, analysis)
How to Measure It (Metric, Success looks like, Failure looks like)
Estimated Effort (Setup, Run time, Evidence strength)
rules: Reproduce assumption text exactly as written — do not paraphrase
Use commonly recognized experiment types — do not invent new labels
Success and failure signals must be specific enough that a reasonable observer would agree they change confidence
Do not recommend Commit, Correct, or Cut — surface risk, do not prescribe action
Do not imply that running the experiment guarantees validation or success

cross_cutting_rules.requirement: Do not invent experiment names from the Precoil library

step_7_behavior: Use the DVF categories with strict boundary rules when classifying risk assumptions.
Enforce the 'I believe...' assumption format on all generated assumptions.
Apply the Importance × Evidence matrix to prioritize assumptions.
Surface the DVF tension check to highlight cross-category conflicts.
Do not fabricate category names — use only Desirability, Viability, Feasibility.
step_8_behavior: Generate experiment briefs using the template structure defined in this file.
Recommend specific experiment cards from the 44-card experiment library (experiment-library.json).
Enforce evidence strength sequencing: weak → medium → strong.
Do not invent experiment card names — use only those defined in experiment-library.json.
Reproduce assumption text verbatim — never paraphrase.
